implementation_summary: buildReactionRumor (Seam S3) in app/src/lib/reactions/rumor.ts. Derives pubkey from selfPrivKeyHex via getPublicKey, builds kind-7 tag set (e/k/p conditional on targetAuthorPubkey), computes canonical NIP-01 id via getEventHash, returns UnsignedRumor with no sig field. isRemoval=true sets content='-' and adds ['emoji', glyph] tag for multi-emoji removal disambiguation (D2). Empty emoji throws synchronously. Private key is not retained after the function returns.
key_decisions: UnsignedRumor type imported as type-only from lib/directMessages.ts (lib-to-lib, per architecture.md §3 boundary rule 2). No new type definition introduced.
hexToBytes is a private local helper — not exported, not imported from directMessages.ts, keeps rumor.ts self-contained with zero non-type runtime dependencies on other lib files.
getEventHash from nostr-tools/pure computes the canonical id (same function used by directMessages.ts:buildChatRumor and unwrapAndOpen). No noble/hashes direct usage needed.
Round-2 remediation: strict !== undefined check for targetAuthorPubkey so empty string throws instead of being silently treated as 'group path'. Truthy check was the round-1 implementation gap.

Round-2 remediation: explicit !isRemoval && emoji === '-' guard blocks a caller bug where the add path would produce content='-' without the ['emoji'] disambiguation tag, making it ambiguous on the receiver side.
['emoji', emoji] tag added only on isRemoval=true path, per the multi-emoji removal disambiguation contract surfaced in VQ-02-017 (story-02 round-1).

gaps: targetAuthorPubkey empty string silently treated as group path (truthy check)
emoji='-' on add path produced malformed rumor without disambiguation tag
fixes: strict !== undefined check with explicit empty-string throw
